Myanmar junta chief and President U Min Aung Hlaing will visit India from May 30–June 3 in his first foreign trip since Myanmar’s controversial 2025–26 elections.

He is scheduled to visit Bodh Gaya, meet PM Narendra Modi, and attend a business forum in Delhi.

Myanmar’s exiled National Unity Government (NUG) has strongly opposed the visit, calling Hlaing a “terrorist junta leader” and urging India not to legitimise the military regime.

Talks are expected to cover border security, insurgency, scam networks, trade connectivity, and the India–Myanmar–Thailand Trilateral Highway project.

- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iranian negotiators signaled openness to transferring the country’s highly enriched uranium stockpile to China, seeking Beijing’s security guarantees as part of a potential nuclear agreement with the US.

● Partial restoration of internet connectivity began in Iran on day 88 of near-total blackout — the longest nationwide internet shutdown in the country’s history.

● UKMTO reported an incident 60 nautical miles east of Muscat, Oman. A vessel suffered an external explosion on the port side near the waterline, with some bunker fuel discharged into the sea. The crew is safe.

● The IDF dismantled ~11km of underground tunnel routes east of the Yellow Line in the Beit Hanoun area in northern Gaza, a former Hamas terrorist stronghold.

● The US Navy resumed escorting commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz under the revived “Project Freedom.” A Greek supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of crude was the first vessel escorted.

● Trump urged Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, and Jordan to join the Abraham Accords and normalize ties with Israel. Pakistan rejected the invitation.

While struggling to understand the structure of Benzene, August Kekulé dreamt of a snake biting its own tail, the ancient Ouroboros symbol.

That vision inspired the idea that benzene’s carbon atoms form a closed ring, not a straight chain. Chemistry changed forever.

Though that image isn’t related it prompted me to share that story with all of you.

Centre Orders Crackdown on Illegal Structures Along India–Pakistan Border

The Government has directed demolition of all unauthorized constructions, especially within 15 km of the India–Pakistan border in Rajasthan, under a “zero-tolerance” security policy.

During a high-level review in Bikaner, Home Minister Amit Shah ordered intensified action against: 
- Infiltration
- Terror financing
- Narcotics smuggling
- Fake Aadhaar networks
- Shell companies & mule accounts

The move comes amid continued security concerns following last year’s India–Pakistan conflict after the Pahalgam terror attack.
